steps.title: Identify the sentence structure
steps.content: The decisive clue is the positioning of “though” between an introductory infinitive phrase (“To manifest...”) and the main clause (“she occasionally relies...”). The word “though” must function as a transitional element connecting these parts.

steps.title: Apply the parenthetical punctuation rule
steps.content: When a transitional word like “though” interrupts between an introductory phrase and a main clause, it must be enclosed by commas on both sides to mark it as a parenthetical element. Option A correctly applies this rule: work, though, creates the proper punctuation. Option B, C, and D violate this punctuation rule—B drops the closing comma, C misapplies a semicolon before a non-independent phrase, and D incorrectly places a semicolon after “though.” That matches choice A — A is correct.
